title: What can wait until tomorrow?
subtitle: A journal set for distinguishing what needs to happen tonight from what the anxious mind has marked as urgent.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for Day 7 of the SLEEP program. Four prompts for separating actual tonight-urgency from the habitual sense that everything is pressing, so the mind can close.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the evening when the mind refuses to rest because the list feels too long or the tasks too unfinished.

content.text: A journal set for Day 7 of the SLEEP program: sorting what requires tonight from what the anxious mind has labelled urgent. The prompts take about fifteen minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What is on my mind that feels like it needs to be done tonight?
Write the full list. Everything the mind is holding. Get it out of the loop.
What on that list would actually become a problem if I left it until tomorrow?
Be specific. "Someone will be harmed" or "a deadline will pass" counts. "I will feel anxious" does not — that is already happening.
What does the anxious part of me believe will happen if I stop for the night?
Write the fear. Not what is likely — what the fear says. Then write one sentence in response to it.
Go back to the first list. Cross off everything that is not on the second list. Write what remains.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
